item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The interview was reasonably effective at eliciting an emotional response (likely due to the content of it being surprisingly believable and something I could easily imagine as a reader), though it didn't really develop much of a character or go anywhere that hadn't already been covered by the description.

The other narrative focus was noticeably weaker, especially as it was almost entirely told through a single, overly-expository letter at the end. It attempts to bring a whole new angle to this article, but without either proper justification for suddenly introducing a new take or enough development for it to not feel entirely bolted on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it, but I did notice:

SCP-4210-C will act in a way similar to children or teenagers, depending on its age and gender. Instances under the age of five are completely dependent on SCP-4210-B for sustenance. Physical fights between older instances of SCP-4210-C/D are common.

I'm pretty sure you meant to say "SCP-4210-C/D will act in a way similar to children or teenagers" but as it stands, SCP-4210-D is not described.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks a bunch for this really appreciate it. One thing though that I have changed was your addition of the exiled instance at chance being slaughtered and served as dinner. While that was my original draft it isn't that now and feels a little ham-fisted with just a one time mention that "hey these guys murder btw" and its never talked about again. Other than that great work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I trimmed it down a bit, mostly taking out repetitive stuff or things that didn't do service to the narrative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bit late but I wanted to post my thanks to the people that helped me through many different drafts of this work, the final product is extremely different to what it was at the beginning and I thank not_a_seagull immensely for that.

Also thanks to AKFrost, TheBlueHour and DrChandra for their critiques earlier on in the drafting process.

In case anyone was wondering the inspiration for this article came from about four of my mates living in houses that have the exact same floor plan and it's always been a running joke among our mates that some weird stuff happened to make this a popular floor plan. The house in the image is one of these mates and I thank him for allowing me to use the image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't know we could use Google Maps/Earth Images, and that they were CC compliant. I'll remember that Anyways, just enough for me to upvote. I do anticipate the NAS rewrite, though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really creative. Using recent history to build your idea is always a good decision. I felt fully immersed in the story, and I really felt something for the 'exiled' entities, which is rare for an SCP article to do (at least for me). Really great job, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty fun to read and I was honestly interested in what was happening, but you may wanna use more clinical terms to make it seem like an actual report like the rest of the SCPs. But overall, pretty good scip

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Seagull, I'd be happy to let you edit parts to trim it down, because I'm sure there are parts I'm blind to as to their importance overall and as a writer I do often suffer from waffling on. However I have removed two paragraphs that I believe did not have anything to add to the narrative so hopefully that is a step in the right direction

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember looking at this. The main problem is still the length. It takes far too long to get where it needs to go. However, the interview has definitely improved since I’ve seen it.

If you want, I can take a crack at rewriting this, or at least edit it to remove some of the bad parts. This is actually relatively good; it just needs some liposuction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I appreciate the feedback! I'll get onto fixing those few things up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great concept executed generally well (an upvote from me), but the tone can be a bit inconsistent. I've never read a scip report with phrasing like "fenced up" or "kicked out", and your descriptions of subjects' emotional states should probably say that they 'express' or 'claim to' feel certain ways, not that they definitively do.
